args.report:
ADS Employment Offer Letter – Sara Khan, Data Analyst

[Attorney-Review Disclaimer: This draft employment offer letter is a template prepared by an AI assistant based on ADS fixed defaults and general principles of Pakistan labour law. It is not legal advice. It must be reviewed, customized, and approved by a qualified attorney licensed in Pakistan (and any other relevant jurisdiction) before use or signature. No party should rely on this draft without such review.]

[ADS Letterhead]
Analytics Dojo SMC Private ("ADS")  
[ADS Registered Address]  
[City], Pakistan  
[Phone] | [Email] | [Website]

[Date: [BLANK]]

Sara Khan  
[Address Line 1: [BLANK]]  
[Address Line 2 / City / Postal Code: [BLANK]]  
[Email: [BLANK]]

Subject: Offer of Employment – Data Analyst

Dear Sara,

ADS is pleased to offer you employment in the position of **Data Analyst** (the "Position"), on the terms and conditions set out in this letter (the "Offer Letter"). This Offer Letter reflects ADS’s standard employment terms, including ADS fixed defaults and provisions intended to align with applicable labour laws of Pakistan.

1. Position, Duties, and Reporting

1.1 **Position.** You will be employed as a **Data Analyst**.

1.2 **Duties.** You will perform the duties and responsibilities customarily associated with the role of a data analyst in a technology / analytics company, including but not limited to: data collection, cleaning, analysis, visualization, reporting, and collaboration with product, engineering, and business teams, as reasonably assigned by ADS from time to time. You agree to perform your duties diligently, competently, and in good faith, and to comply with all lawful and reasonable directions, policies, and procedures of ADS.

1.3 **Reporting Line.** You will report to **[BLANK – e.g., Head of Analytics / Line Manager Name]** or such other person as ADS may designate from time to time.

1.4 **Exclusivity and Best Efforts.** During your employment, you will devote your full working time and attention to ADS’s business and will not, without ADS’s prior written consent, engage in any other employment, consultancy, or business activity (whether full-time or part-time) that conflicts or may reasonably be expected to conflict with your duties to ADS, subject always to applicable Pakistan labour law protections.

2. Start Date, Location, and Probation

2.1 **Start Date.** Your employment is expected to commence on **[Start Date: [BLANK]]**, or such other date as may be mutually agreed in writing.

2.2 **Work Location.** Your primary work location will be **[City, Pakistan: [BLANK]]**, at ADS’s offices or such other location (including remote or hybrid arrangements) as ADS may reasonably determine, subject to applicable law.

2.3 **Probation Period (Pakistan Labour Law Default).** Your employment will be subject to a probationary period of **[Probation Duration: [BLANK – e.g., up to 3–6 months, consistent with local law and company policy]]** commencing from your Start Date. During probation, either you or ADS may terminate the employment by giving the notice specified in Section 10, subject to applicable labour laws of Pakistan and any mandatory statutory protections. Upon successful completion of probation, your employment will continue on the terms of this Offer Letter, subject to any updated written contract or policy.

3. Compensation and Benefits

3.1 **Base Salary.** Your gross base salary will be **PKR [BLANK] per month** ("Base Salary"), payable in arrears in accordance with ADS’s standard payroll practices and applicable law, subject to all required deductions and withholdings under Pakistan law.

3.2 **Variable Compensation / Bonus.** You may be eligible to participate in ADS’s discretionary bonus or incentive schemes, if any, as may be introduced or amended by ADS from time to time. Any such bonus is not guaranteed unless expressly confirmed in writing, and will be subject to the applicable plan rules, your performance, ADS’s performance, and your continued employment on the payment date.

3.3 **Benefits.** You will be eligible to participate in ADS’s standard employee benefits programs applicable to your position and location, as may be in effect from time to time, including any health insurance, provident fund, gratuity, or other benefits required or permitted under Pakistan law and ADS policy. Details of current benefits will be provided separately and may be updated by ADS in its discretion, subject to applicable law.

3.4 **Statutory Contributions and Deductions.** ADS will make all mandatory employer contributions and deductions as required by applicable Pakistan labour and social security laws, and will deduct from your compensation all taxes and other amounts required by law.

4. Working Hours, Leave, and Holidays

4.1 **Working Hours.** Your normal working hours will be **[BLANK – e.g., 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., Monday to Friday]**, with such reasonable additional hours as may be necessary for the proper performance of your duties, subject to applicable working time and overtime rules under Pakistan labour law.

4.2 **Weekly Rest and Public Holidays.** You will be entitled to weekly rest days and public holidays in accordance with applicable Pakistan labour laws and ADS policy.

4.3 **Annual Leave.** You will be entitled to paid annual leave of **[BLANK – e.g., number of days per year consistent with Pakistan law and ADS policy]**, accrued and taken in accordance with ADS policy and applicable law.

4.4 **Sick Leave and Other Leave.** You will be entitled to sick leave, maternity / paternity leave, and any other statutory leave as required under Pakistan labour law, and any additional leave benefits that ADS may provide under its policies from time to time.

5. Market-Standard Benchmarks and Jurisdiction-Specific Rules

5.1 **Market-Standard Benchmarks.** ADS aims to maintain compensation and benefits that are competitive with market-standard benchmarks for similar roles in the Pakistan technology and analytics sector, taking into account experience, performance, and internal equity. However, nothing in this Offer Letter guarantees any particular market position or future adjustments, which remain at ADS’s discretion, subject to applicable law.

5.2 **Jurisdiction-Specific Compliance.** This Offer Letter is intended to comply with applicable labour, employment, and social security laws of Pakistan. To the extent any provision of this Offer Letter conflicts with a mandatory requirement of Pakistan law, the mandatory requirement will prevail, and the conflicting provision will be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to comply with such law while preserving, as closely as possible, the parties’ original intent.

6. Confidentiality, IP, and Flow-Down Obligations

6.1 **Confidential Information.** You acknowledge that in the course of your employment you will have access to confidential and proprietary information of ADS and its clients, partners, and affiliates ("Confidential Information"). You agree to keep all such Confidential Information strictly confidential, to use it only for the proper performance of your duties, and not to disclose it to any third party except as authorized by ADS or required by law.

6.2 **Intellectual Property.** All work product, data models, code, analyses, reports, documentation, and other materials that you create, develop, or contribute to in the course of your employment ("Work Product") will be the exclusive property of ADS to the maximum extent permitted by law. You hereby assign to ADS all rights, title, and interest in and to such Work Product, including all intellectual property rights, and agree to execute any documents reasonably required to perfect or enforce such rights.

6.3 **Flow-Down Obligations.** You agree to comply with any confidentiality, data protection, security, and other obligations that ADS is required to "flow down" to its personnel under contracts with clients, partners, or other third parties, to the extent such obligations are communicated to you in writing or via ADS policies or training. These obligations may include restrictions on data use, data export, information security, and professional conduct.

7. Non-Competition, Non-Solicitation, and NNN Matrix

7.1 **Non-Competition (Subject to Pakistan Law).** To the extent permitted by applicable Pakistan law, during your employment you will not engage in any business or activity that competes with ADS’s business or that could reasonably be expected to conflict with your duties to ADS, without ADS’s prior written consent.

7.2 **Non-Solicitation.** During your employment and for **[BLANK – e.g., 6–12 months]** following the termination of your employment (the "Restricted Period"), you will not, directly or indirectly, (a) solicit or attempt to solicit any employee or contractor of ADS to terminate or reduce their relationship with ADS, or (b) solicit or attempt to solicit business from any client or prospective client of ADS with whom you had material dealings in the 12 months preceding termination, in each case to the extent permitted by applicable law.

7.3 **Non-Disclosure, Non-Use, Non-Circumvention (NNN Matrix).** You acknowledge and agree that:

(a) **Non-Disclosure.** You will not disclose any Confidential Information of ADS or its clients to any third party, except as expressly authorized by ADS or required by law.

(b) **Non-Use.** You will not use any Confidential Information for any purpose other than the proper performance of your duties for ADS.

(c) **Non-Circumvention.** You will not, during your employment and for the Restricted Period, directly or indirectly circumvent ADS by using ADS’s Confidential Information, client relationships, or business opportunities for your own benefit or for the benefit of any third party, in a manner that competes with or harms ADS, to the extent permitted by applicable law.

8. Data Protection and Information Security

8.1 **Personal Data.** ADS will process your personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws and ADS’s privacy and HR policies, as updated from time to time.

8.2 **Information Security.** You agree to comply with all ADS information security, acceptable use, and data handling policies, including any client-specific security requirements that apply to your work.

9. Policies, Code of Conduct, and Structural Repairs

9.1 **Policies and Code of Conduct.** You agree to comply with all ADS policies, procedures, and codes of conduct in force from time to time, including those relating to anti-harassment, anti-discrimination, anti-bribery and corruption, information security, and workplace health and safety.

9.2 **Structural Repairs and Updates to Terms.** ADS may from time to time make structural updates or "repairs" to its standard employment terms and policies (for example, to reflect changes in law, market practice, or internal governance). Such updates may be implemented through revised policies, employee handbooks, or updated contract terms, subject to applicable law and any required notice or consent. Where a change materially affects your terms and conditions of employment, ADS will provide you with written notice and, where required by law, obtain your consent or execute an amendment.

10. Term and Termination

10.1 **Term.** Your employment is on an ongoing basis and will continue until terminated by either party in accordance with this Section 10 and applicable Pakistan labour law.

10.2 **Notice of Termination.** After completion of the probation period, either you or ADS may terminate the employment by giving **[Notice Period: [BLANK – e.g., 30 days]]** written notice or payment in lieu of notice, where permitted by law. During probation, the notice period will be **[BLANK – e.g., shorter period consistent with law and policy]**, subject to applicable statutory minimums.

10.3 **Termination for Cause.** ADS may terminate your employment without notice or with shorter notice where permitted by applicable law, including for serious misconduct, gross negligence, fraud, willful disobedience of lawful orders, or other grounds recognized under Pakistan labour law.

10.4 **Final Settlement.** Upon termination of your employment, ADS will pay you all salary and benefits due up to your last working day, together with any statutory entitlements (such as accrued but unused leave, gratuity, or other benefits) in accordance with applicable law and ADS policy.

11. Return of Property and Continuing Obligations

11.1 **Return of Property.** Upon termination of your employment for any reason, or upon ADS’s earlier request, you will promptly return to ADS all property belonging to ADS or its clients, including laptops, devices, documents, data, and any copies thereof, in whatever form.

11.2 **Continuing Obligations.** Your obligations under Sections 6 (Confidentiality, IP, and Flow-Down Obligations), 7 (Non-Competition, Non-Solicitation, and NNN Matrix), 8 (Data Protection and Information Security), and 11 (Return of Property and Continuing Obligations) will survive termination of your employment to the extent permitted by applicable law.

12. Entire Agreement, Amendments, and Ratification

12.1 **Entire Agreement.** This Offer Letter, together with any documents expressly incorporated by reference (including ADS policies and any separate confidentiality or IP agreements you may sign), constitutes the entire agreement between you and ADS regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous understandings, whether written or oral, relating to such subject matter, except where otherwise required by mandatory law.

12.2 **Amendments.** Any amendment or modification to this Offer Letter must be in writing and signed by both you and an authorized representative of ADS, except where ADS is permitted by law to update certain terms via policy changes as described in Section 9.2.

12.3 **Ratification Clause (Amendments and Sub-Arrangements).** Without limiting Section 12.2, if ADS enters into any amendment, side letter, or sub-arrangement relating to your employment (including, for example, changes to reporting lines, secondments, or assignments to affiliates or client sites), such document will:

(a) identify the specific provisions of this Offer Letter that are being varied or supplemented;  
(b) state whether it is intended to be temporary or permanent; and  
(c) upon signature by you and ADS, be deemed to ratify and confirm this Offer Letter as amended, with all unaffected provisions continuing in full force and effect.

To the extent any prior informal understanding or practice is inconsistent with this Offer Letter or a later written amendment, the written document will prevail, and by signing you ratify and confirm that understanding.

13. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution

13.1 **Governing Law.** This Offer Letter and any dispute arising out of or in connection with it will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Pakistan, without giving effect to any conflict of laws principles.

13.2 **Dispute Resolution.** The parties will first attempt in good faith to resolve any dispute arising out of or relating to this Offer Letter through informal discussions. If the dispute cannot be resolved amicably within a reasonable period, it may be referred to the competent courts or labour tribunals of **[City / Province, Pakistan: [BLANK]]**, subject to any mandatory jurisdictional rules under Pakistan law.

14. Acceptance of Offer

If you wish to accept this Offer, please sign and date the acknowledgement below and return a scanned copy to ADS by **[Acceptance Deadline: [BLANK]]**, or as otherwise agreed in writing. This Offer is conditional upon:

(a) your confirmation that you are legally entitled to work in Pakistan and to perform the duties of the Position;  
(b) satisfactory completion of any background checks or reference checks permitted by law and conducted in accordance with ADS policy; and  
(c) your agreement to comply with ADS’s policies and procedures, as updated from time to time, subject to applicable law.

We look forward to welcoming you to Analytics Dojo SMC Private and to your contributions as a Data Analyst.

Yours sincerely,

For and on behalf of  
**Analytics Dojo SMC Private**

Name: [BLANK]  
Title: [BLANK]  
Date: [BLANK]


Acknowledged and Agreed:

I, **Sara Khan**, hereby accept the above offer of employment with Analytics Dojo SMC Private on the terms and conditions set out in this Offer Letter and confirm that I have read and understood them. I acknowledge that I have been advised to obtain independent legal advice before signing.

Signature: ___________________________  
Name: Sara Khan  
Date: ___________________________
